items.title: First round of US-Iran talks ends with encouraging progress, mediators say
items.summary: The US-Iran talks began on Sunday in Switzerland, after last week's agreement, which includes a commitment to reach a final deal within 60 days.

items.title: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
items.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella appears to have narrowly defeated his rival Iván Cepeda, who says the preliminary count is "not yet official or binding".

items.title: Largest ever cocaine bust in Australia after police raid underground bunker
items.summary: Police seized 2.7 tonnes of cocaine worth an estimated A$816m after searching a property in western Sydney.

items.title: Russian troop build-up threatens city seen as key to seizing Ukraine's Donbas
items.summary: If Kostyantynivka falls, Russian forces would be able push towards Ukraine's last remaining strongholds in the east.

items.title: Alan Greenspan, architect of the modern American economy, dies aged 100
items.summary: As chairman of the Federal Reserve, Alan Greenspan became the world's most high-profile banker.

items.title: Former Kenyan justice minister blocked from entering Uganda, lawyers' body says
items.summary: Martha  Karua is a lawyer representing detained Ugandan opposition leader Kizza Besigye who is on trial for treason.

items.title: Schools close as more than half of France under red heat alerts
items.summary: The health minister warns many citizens "will suffer", with temperatures set to peak on Monday.

items.title: Japan quintuples visa fees in first price hike since 1978
items.summary: Authorities say they do not expect the hikes to have an "immediate impact on inbound tourism".

items.title: Late singer Oliver Tree's body returned to US after helicopter crash
items.summary: The 32-year-old California native died whilst on his world tour, ahead of a date in Brazil.

items.title: Three dead in Philippines high school shooting over bullying 'grudge'
items.summary: Police say they believe the attack was motivated by a "grudge" over bullying.

items.title: Cape Verde fan goes wild live on BBC News as his country scores
items.summary: During a live BBC News broadcast, reporter Paul Njie interviews a Cape Verde fan when the country suddenly scores its first ever World Cup goal against Uruguay in the 2026 World Cup.

items.title: Trump says repairs to algae-plagued Reflecting Pool will begin immediately
items.summary: Despite a multi-million dollar renovation, the Washington DC landmark has peeling paint and algae. Trump claims vandals are partly to blame.

items.title: China's import of custard apples is sparking fears in Taiwan
items.summary: Taiwan's agriculture ministry is worried that Beijing wants to weaponise a local specialty: the atemoya.

items.title: Tight security as Indian students resit medical exam after alleged paper leak
items.summary: Millions of candidates face biometric checks and frisking as air force deployed to secure exam papers.

items.title: Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say
items.summary: The Israeli military accused Ahmed Wishah of being a "Hamas sniper operative", without providing evidence.

items.title: Dutch royals enjoy two big results in one World Cup day
items.summary: There may be about 5,000 miles between the Netherlands and their former colony Curacao but members of the Dutch royal family managed to see both sides play on Saturday.

items.title: Former Olympian denies vandalising Washington Reflecting Pool after arrest
items.summary: Davey Hearn says he was simply touching the new paint at the site out of curiosity and did not remove or alter it.

items.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
items.summary: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

items.title: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
items.summary: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

items.title: Indian cricketer, 15, hits record-breaking 50 in just 11 balls
items.summary: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaks the record for the fastest 50-over List A half-century in history on the same day he was not included in India's one-day international squad.

items.title: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour after award stripped
items.summary: Ukraine's president said his country was open to "engagement" about "difficult and painful chapters of our shared past".

items.title: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike
items.summary: Mona Khalil, who had refused to leave the beach she had spent years protecting, died after several days in hospital, an environmental group said.

items.title: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
items.summary: More than eight million South Africans are living with HIV – the highest number of any country in the world.

items.title: Australia confirms first case of H5N1 bird flu as virus reaches every continent
items.summary: Australia was previously the only continent where the H5N1 bird flu strain had not yet been found.

items.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
items.summary: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.

items.title: Tourist dies in Dominican Republic luxury resort fire
items.summary: Nearly 1,700 people were evacuated from the huge blaze at Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach in Bayahibe, a popular resort town on the Caribbean coast.

items.title: James Burrows, legendary director of Cheers and Friends, dies aged 85
items.summary: Best-known as co-creator of sitcom Cheers, Burrows directed more than 1,000 episodes of other classics including the Big Bang Theory and Will and Grace.

items.title: Is Germany looking again at coal-powered electricity?
items.summary: It had planned to abandon the fuel, but the higher cost of natural gas may make it think again.

items.title: The BTS fans losing thousands as scammers cash in on comeback tour ticket war
items.summary: As ticket demand outstrips supply by a ratio of 15 to one, desperate fans have become easy targets.

items.title: Currency crash and visa crackdowns force Indian students to rethink studying abroad
items.summary: A weaker rupee and tougher immigration rules are pushing Indian students away from popular destinations like the US and UK.

items.title: Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict
items.summary: Abiy Ahmed's party retains its huge majority despite unrest in several parts of Ethiopia and tensions with its neighbours.

items.title: Backstage at Gorillaz' epic, one-off stadium show: 'The vibe is ridiculous'
items.summary: Damon Albarn, De La Soul and Moonchild Sannelly talk backstage as Gorillaz play their biggest show.

items.title: At CrimeCon true crime obsessives come face-to-face with real loss
items.summary: Victims' families say there are ways to be an ethical true-crime fan - not an exploitative one.

items.title: Colombia's escalating, brutal internal conflict is defining its presidential election
items.summary: A left-wing senator who backs talks with armed gangs faces an outsider endorsed by Trump.

items.title: Steve Rosenberg: Moscow oil refinery attack brings Russia's war with Ukraine closer to home
items.summary: There are moments when life in Moscow feels completely normal - Thursday morning wasn't one of them, writes Steve Rosenberg.

items.title: Why an AI company cleaned my New York City apartment for free
items.summary: An AI company is sending free cleaners door-to-door in a bid to train the robots it hopes one day will replace them.

items.title: Japanese pop group XG went from brutal five year training to global pop stars
items.summary: The Japanese pop group have become global stars, after being recruited before they were teenagers.

items.title: Thousands killed in US-Israeli war on Iran - but experts say true total may never be known
items.summary: Experts say internet, media and government restrictions have all hampered casualty reporting across the region.

items.title: What Iran and US get from deal and why both could struggle to keep it
items.summary: BBC analysts assess the claims by both Washington and Tehran to have won a victory with the deal to end their war.

items.title: Weapons, money and ships: How is this Iran deal different from others?
items.summary: BBC Verify examines how the new deal between the US and Iran affects three key areas.

items.title: US-Iran deal leaves core sticking points unresolved - and a $300bn question
items.summary: Trump has insisted the deal ensures that Iran will never buy, develop or produce a nuclear weapon. But text of the agreement falls short of that.

items.title: Parisians cool off in canal amid 'red alert' heatwave
items.summary: France has issued red heatwave alerts for around half the country as temperatures soar to record levels.

items.title: BBC sees destroyed villages in Israeli-occupied southern Lebanon
items.summary: Travelling with a humanitarian convoy, BBC's Hugo Bachega has been given rare access to a part of Lebanon under Israeli occupation.

items.title: Would you choose to take a 22-hour non-stop flight?
items.summary: The BBC asked Sydney locals if they would take the newly announced, longest ever commercial flight from Sydney to London.

items.title: Should the US impose a teen social media ban like the UK?
items.summary: The UK is enforcing a social media ban for children under the age of 16. The BBC asked Americans of different ages if they thought it was a good idea.

items.title: Will Putin change tactics after Ukrainian drone attacks?
items.summary: Steve Rosenberg explains what Russia's next steps may be after frequent Ukrainian drone attacks.

items.title: Moment Trump signs US-Iran agreement at Palace of Versailles
items.summary: The 14-point Memorandum of Understanding was signed at a post-G7 dinner in France.

items.title: 'Warning fire went up': Couple on board yacht describe encounter with Russian warship
items.summary: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.

items.title: Drones create the first-ever Fifa scoreboard in Seattle sky
items.summary: BBC reporter Max Matza is in Seattle, Washington, where 400 drones lit up the night sky on Monday to display the score of the Egypt v Belgium match.
